uiMode.advancedHint: All sections are visible — models, MCP servers, prompt templates, tools.
uiMode.simpleHint: Only the essential settings are visible. Switch on advanced to change models, integrations, and prompts.

imageProcessing.title: Image processing
imageProcessing.description: Reflex automatically resizes and re-encodes attached images before saving them to {dir}.
imageProcessing.hint: Saves model tokens and disk space. Vectors (SVG) and animated GIF/WebP are kept as-is.

toolsPolicy.noToolsHint: without them the agent will ask for permission and hang in headless mode

gemini.title: Gemini
gemini.description: Used for YouTube summarization (native multimodal URL support) and for generating images via the Nano Banana model ({model}, ~$0.04 per image). The key is stored locally in {path} (0600), never passed to agents. Grab a key at aistudio.google.com/apikey — there's a free tier (15 RPM / 1500 RPD).
gemini.defaultModelHint: Used for generic Gemini calls when no specific task is set.
gemini.youtubeModelHint: For video summarization. Flash variants are faster and usually smart enough.

imageSearch.title: Images
imageSearch.description: Keys for stock-photo APIs. Used by the "Picture in KB" widget and the utility host API ({api}). Stored locally in {dir} (0600).

unsplash.label: Unsplash
unsplash.hint: Free 50 requests/hour. Author attribution required — the modal inserts it automatically.

pexels.label: Pexels
pexels.hint: No hard limit for reasonable use. Attribution recommended.

brave.label: Brave (whole web)
brave.hint: Image search across the entire web (not just stock). If you already have the Brave Search MCP connected, the key is picked up from there automatically — you can leave this empty.

oauth.title: OAuth providers
oauth.description: Reflex stores access/refresh tokens locally and refreshes them on expiry. Use $oauth:<provider> in MCP server env/headers — Reflex substitutes a fresh token on every call. Redirect URI: {uri}.
oauth.forgetClientConfirm: Delete the entire OAuth config for {label} (client_id, secret and tokens)?
oauth.removeProviderConfirm: Delete custom provider "{id}" entirely (along with client + tokens)?
oauth.usageHint: Use $oauth:{id} in any MCP server env/headers — Reflex injects a fresh access_token on every call (with auto-refresh).

custom.title: Custom OAuth provider
custom.description: Any OAuth 2.0 service with a standard Authorization Code flow. Redirect URI: {uri}.
custom.setupHintPlaceholder: Go to Dropbox App Console → Create App → Permission type: Scoped access. Redirect URI: http://localhost:3210/api/oauth/callback.
custom.extraMustBeJson: extraAuthorizeParams must be a JSON object

mcpServers.title: MCP servers
mcpServers.description: Registered MCP servers are available to utilities (via manifest.mcpServers) and directly to chat (the orchestrator gets them via --mcp-config). Config is stored at {path} with perms 0600.
mcpServers.wizardDescription: Describe it in plain language ("connect google calendar", "add github mcp"). The agent finds the package, explains where to grab the token / start the OAuth flow, and produces a ready-to-go card — you just approve it.
mcpServers.wizardOpenedToast: Opening chat with the installer agent
mcpServers.wizardFooter: A chat will open in your first project — the agent will ask follow-ups and propose a config. The config is saved to the registry after you click "Register".
mcpServers.emptyHint: No MCP servers yet. Add your first one — it will become available to utilities (via manifest.mcpServers) and chat.

form.title: New MCP server
form.testFirstHint: First click "Check" to verify the server starts.

mapServices.title: Routing services
mapServices.description: Which navigation services show up in the "Route to…" popup on each point of the map widget. Reflex generates a deep-link from the coordinates — no intermediaries, no accounts.
mapServices.cardHint: Selected services will appear in the popup on each point of the map widget — "Route to…". The link is generated straight from the coordinates, no intermediaries. You can preview the link (default point is the center of Moscow).

ngrok.title: Public links (ngrok)
ngrok.description: Make a utility, KB file, or dashboard accessible from the internet via an ngrok tunnel. Each link can be password-protected; middleware blocks all other paths on the ngrok host.
ngrok.intro: Reflex runs ngrok http with your token; only paths {path} are allowed on the ngrok domain. Install the ngrok CLI in PATH before launching.
ngrok.authtokenHint: Find it in <link>ngrok dashboard → Your Authtoken</link>. Stored at {path} (0600).
ngrok.apiKeyLabel: API key (for the domain list, optional)
ngrok.apiKeyHint: Grab it from <link>dashboard → API → API keys</link>. Used only for the GET request listing reserved domains.
ngrok.restartTitle: Stop and start again with the current settings
ngrok.portMismatch: Tunnel is listening on port {running}, settings say {desired}.
ngrok.noShares: Nothing shared yet. A "Share" button will appear on the utility, KB file, or dashboard page.
